description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a combination of rough and flat surfaces. The rock displays a natural, weathered texture on most sides, with one side appearing freshly broken and smoother, revealing a lighter interior. The coloration ranges from light gray to brownish-gray, with some subtle speckling and a visible label or marking on one of the flatter surfaces. The edges are jagged and angular, suggesting it was fractured from a larger piece. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural obstacle or barrier in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside.
description: A jagged, medium-sized rock with rough, weathered surfaces and a lighter, freshly broken face, featuring a visible label.
description_view_0: The rock's rough, brownish-gray surface and jagged edges are prominent, with a natural, weathered appearance.
description_view_1: A smoother, lighter gray face is visible, showing a freshly broken surface and a partial view of the label.
description_view_2: The flat, light gray surface dominates, with the label 'VEFLAT' clearly visible and rougher texture on the lower edge.
description_view_3: A side view highlighting the transition between the rough, textured surface and the smoother, broken face, with the label partially visible.
